observations.recommendation: 8. For local DRRM Officer, report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (DRRMF) – on or before 15th day after the end of each month.

9. We recommended that Management furnish the copies of the contract documents and POs not later than five (5) working days from its perfection, together with its duly accomplished supporting documents, for technical review and evaluation in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.
15. Prepare a lapsing schedule records for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

21. We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to jointly work on the analysis/review of the accounts, if it is not possible due to the absence of records and documents, the municipal accountant should request for write-off and/or adjustments of account balances from the COA.

1.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Hire/appoint General Services Officer who shall take custody of
and be accountable for all properties, real or personal, owned by
the Municipality; and
b) Create an Inventory Committee who shall conduct a complete
physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality and
prepare a report thereon in accordance with Section 124 and 114
of NGAS Manual for LGUs to determine the existence and
accuracy of PPE.

2.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Acting
Municipal Treasurer to:
a) Stop the practice of using cash advance to pay for the
Municipality’s operating expenses and activities;
b) Ensure that payments for suppliers are made thru checks;
c) Ensure that the purposes of cash advances are clearly stated on
the face of the disbursement vouchers and that these are duly
supported with complete documents at the time of grant of cash
advances; and
d) Discontinue the practice of granting multiple cash advances.

3.12 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct the concerned Municipality personnel, in coordination
with the BAC to meticulously and judiciously plan the
Municipality’s Annual Procurement;
b) Direct the concerned Municipality personnel, in coordination
with the BAC, to meticulously and judiciously plan the
Municipality's Annual Procurement to avoid inconvenience and
significant delays of the implementation of the Municipality’s
Project, Program and Activities thereby improving the
Municipality government’s delivery of public service;
c) Direct the BAC Secretariat to fully utilize the PlilGEPS via
posting the necessary Invitation to Bid. Notice of Award.
Perfected Contract and Notice to Proceed for each of the
Municipality's procurement to promote transparency and
efficiency;
d) Direct the BAC Secretariat to meet its function and responsibility
as the main support unit of the BAC for efficient processing of
the Municipality’s procurement;
e) Direct the Technical Working Group to properly evaluate the
financial capability;
f) Direct the Technical Working Group to properly evaluate the
financial, technical and legal capability of the bidders to provide the HoPE with an appropriate recommendation of determining a
qualified supplier, contractor or consultant;
g) Direct the BAC to properly and completely fill out the documents
related to proçurement and to avoid tampering of such to shed
out doubt as to authenticity of the said documents: and
h) Direct the BAC and all the Municipality personnel to strictly
adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its 2016 RIRR for the
Municipality to meet the principles of transparency,
accountability, equity, efficiency and economy in its procurement
process.

5.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following:
a) Use of required format of sequentially numbered trip ticket duly
approved by the Agency Head or the authorized representative
for proper monitoring of fuel consumption with complete
information being filled out in every detail needed to support the
claim for payment of fuel expenses;
b) Enforce the “No Driver’s Trip Ticket, No Withdrawal Policy;” and
c) Install controls in the monitoring of issuance of the fuel and
strictly adhere to the guidelines on the use of government
vehicles.

6.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Require the GSO-designate to:
   i. Maintain property cards (PC) for the PPEs to account for
   the receipts and dispositions thereof; and
   ii. Ensure that the deliveries of properties and equipment are
   immediately recorded in the property cards (PC) based on
   the AIR and other supporting documents;
b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
   i. Maintain ledger cards for each category of PPEs;
   ii. Make a reconciliation between the inventory reports,
   property cards, and subsidiary ledgers;
   iii. Ensure that the deliveries of properties and equipment are
   immediately recorded in the PPE Subsidiary Ledger Cards
   based on the AIR and other supporting documents and
   ensure strict adherence to the above-stated laws, rules, and
   regulations.

7.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Inspectorate Team/Committee to furnish the COA Audit Team copies
of AIRs within 24 hours after the acceptance and inspection of
deliveries so that timely inspection thereof can be conducted by the
Auditors.

ensuing month; and

or before 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the BRS and its supporting documents monthly to the Auditor, within ten days after receipt of the Bank Statements in compliance with the Section 74 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that the LGU formulate LDPC and LIPC and utilize and implement LCPC Fund in accordance with identified PPAs as provided in DILG MC No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

We recommended that Management strictly implement the mandatory deductions pertaining to GSIS, Philhealth and withholding tax for personnel and remitting said deductions on time in compliance with the above-cited provisions of laws and regulations.

We recommended that: a. The Municipality of Binidayan employees should file their membership to PAG-IBIG; b. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should deduct the mandatory PAG-IBIG contributions and remit monthly contributions promptly respectively to the said Fund.

We recommend that|management should|conduct physical|inventory of properties,|plant and equipment and|submit the related|Report of the Physical|Count on the Property,|Plant and Equipment|(RPCPPE) to the Auditor|for verification in|compliance with Section|124 of the NGAS Manual|for Local Government|Units (LGUs), Volume I.|Further, we recommend|that the Accounting and|General Services Office|to complete and update|their property|records/ledger card and|reconcile existing|records.
observations.managementAction: Physical|inventory was|conducted by the|management and|inventory reports were|submitted.
observations.reason: There are other|reports|unsubmitted for|reconciliation|with the records|of the supply|officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the|Municipal Account be|directed to make the|necessary adjustments in|the books of accounts|for the transfer of the|subject PPE accounts|from the Trust Fund|books to the General|Fund, take up the|unrecognized Income from|Donations and set up the|depreciation expenses.|Prepare the related|Journal Entry Voucher|and submit the same to|the Auditor for audit.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed|the accountant to|transfer subject PPE|from TF to GF and|prepare necessary JEV|and submit the same|to the auditor.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to|the Municipal Accountant|to prepare and submit|monthly bank|reconciliation|statements regularly for|all funds pursuant to|Section 74 of PD 1445|and COA Cir. 96-11.

We recommended that the|municipal accountant and|treasurer should jointly|work on the|analysis/review of the|accounts, if it is not|possible due to the|absence of records and|documents, the municipal|accountant should|request for write-off|and/or adjustments of|account balances from|the COA.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal|Accountant jointly|worked for the|analysis/review of|dormant accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the|Accountable Officer|should prepare and|submit regularly to the|Office of the Auditor|the Monthly Report of|Accountability for|Accountable Forms With|and Without Money Value|to ensure compliance to|Section 98 of PD 1445|and Section 50 of|Manual of the New|Government Accounting|System for Local|Government Units|(MNGAS) Volume II.

Initiate measures|designed to come up|with a Memorandum of|Agreement (MOA) with|the GSIS defining|therein the agreed|balance of payables to|GSIS, if any, and the|terms and conditions in|the settlement thereof.|The following steps|may help in|facilitating|reconciliation of|records. Secure|detailed profile of|payables per GSIS|records; Reconstruct|old GSIS accounts, if|necessary, and update|records; Reconstruct|old personnel profile,|if necessary, and|update personnel|profile; Counter-check|GSIS records with the|reconstructed and|updated accounting and|personnel records of|the LGU; Negotiate|with the GSIS for any|differences that may be|noted during|counter-checking|activity; Enter into a|formal agreement with|the GSIS in the form of|a Memorandum of|Agreement (MOA); and|Appropriate fund for|the settlement of any|payables to the GSIS|as agreed in the MOA.
observations.managementAction: The LGU top management|will Initiate measures|designed to come up|with a Memorandum of|Agreement (MOA) with|the GSIS defining|therein the agreed|balance of payables to|GSIS, if any, and the|terms and conditions in|the settlement thereof.|The following steps may|be done as recommended
observations.reason: The LGU and GSIS|reconciliation of|records is|on-going and had|not agreed on some|terms and|conditions.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal|Accountant and|Accountable Officer to|record and facilitate|the immediate|submission of Reports|of Check Issued (RCI),|Reports of Disbursement|(ROD) and|transactions documents|consisting of paid|vouchers/payrolls|together with|supporting documents|to Auditor’s Office and|strictly adhere to the|provisions of COA|Circular No. 94-013|dated December 13,|1994.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the|accountant to transfer|subject PPE from TF to|GF and prepare|necessary JEV and|submit the same to the|auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that|management should make|sure that observers|(consisting of the|concerned auditor, a|representative of a duly|recognized private group|in a sector or|discipline relevant to|the procurement at hand|and a representative of|a NGO) are duly|notified in writing at|least 2-day before the|stages of procurement|to which observers|shall be invited,|namely: pre-bid|conference; opening of|bids; post-qualification;|contract award; and|special meetings of the|BAC.
observations.managementAction: The LGU will notify the|COA Auditor on stages|of procurement.
observations.reason: Only few|notifications were|received by COA for|CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: Require management to|furnish COA copy of|any contract / purchase|order including all|pertinent supporting|documents as enumerated|under COA Circular|2009-001 dated|February 12, 2009|within 5-day after its|perfection / approval /|execution.
observations.managementAction: The POs and contracts|will be submitted to|COA within 5 days upon|execution thereof.
observations.reason: Only few POs and|contracts were|submitted to COA.
